title: What it actually looks like to need another man
caption:
The most dangerous version of you is the one flying solo and calling it strength.

Most men don't have one man who actually knows them. They have acquaintances. They have group chats. They have people who know the gym version or the Sunday version or the professional version.

None of those is accountability. Accountability is one man who has your number, knows your sin, and picks up the phone.

If you can't name him right now, you don't have him. That's the gap. Start there.

Being known is harder than being liked. Brotherhood happens the first time you say the thing you've never said out loud and the man across the table doesn't flinch. That conversation is the one that builds something real.

You find accountability by being accountable. Get in another man's corner — not because you have it together, but because you don't.

"Confess your sins to one another, that you may be healed." — James 5:16.

The light gets in where you let another man in.

—

Comment "brother" and I'll send you the link to the app.
